header.title: Models
header.subtitle: The signal is built from three layers: Day, Micro, and SL.
header.linkLabel: Detailed metrics are available on a dedicated page.

overview.title: Layer overview
overview.text: Pipeline: Day -> Day+Micro -> Total (with SL overlay).

rows: Is there a move and in which direction

terms.description:
Daily layer (Move + Dir) is the base model of the current forecast.

What it shows:
first the Move layer estimates whether the day is likely to reach [[min-move|MinMove]]. Then the Dir layer decides whether that move is more likely to go up or down.

How to read it:
this is the first and main model answer for the day. All later layers only refine or constrain it.

Move (P(move)) is the probability that the day will reach [[min-move|MinMove]] and deliver enough movement for an entry into a trade.

What it shows:
if this probability is low, the day is closer to [[landing-day-flat|sideways]]. If it is high, the system believes there is enough movement to continue into direction.

How to read it:
Move is not about trade direction. It is only about whether the day is likely to produce a meaningful move at all.

Dir (P(up|move)) is the conditional probability of an upward move once the Move layer already believes the day can reach [[min-move|MinMove]].

What it shows:
Dir does not decide whether movement exists. It chooses the side inside an already expected move: up or down.

How to read it:
Dir only makes sense together with Move. A strong Dir value alone does not make the day tradeable if Move is still weak.

Micro layer (flat-only) is the additional layer that activates only for [[landing-day-flat|sideways]] days.

What it shows:
it looks for a weak tilt up or down inside a day that [[current-prediction-daily-layer|Daily]] did not classify as a full directional scenario.

How to read it:
the micro layer does not replace the daily model. It exists only to refine neutral days.

SL risk layer is the layer that estimates the probability that [[tp-sl|stop-loss]] will hit before [[tp-sl|take-profit]] after entry into a trade.

What it shows:
it does not choose market direction. It evaluates bad-entry risk. If risk is high, the final system cuts position size or can refuse the trade.

How to read it:
if the final forecast looks more cautious than Day + Micro, the reason often sits in the SL risk layer.

Total prediction is the final answer after [[current-prediction-daily-layer|Daily]], the micro layer, and the SL risk layer.

What it shows:
this is no longer an intermediate probability block. It is the final scenario of the day that the trading [[policy|Policy]] reads next.

How to read it:
if one main answer is needed from the system for the day, Total is the correct field to use, while earlier layers explain how that answer was assembled.

daily.title: Daily model
daily.text: Outputs three classes: UP / FLAT / DOWN.

terms.description:
Who defines FLAT — FLAT is defined by baseline-window conditions and runtime probabilities.

What it shows:
this term is used in the explain section as a reading anchor for a specific model step, regime flag, split rule, or feature-calculation block.

How to read it:
its meaning should be read together with the neighboring block and with the way it changes forecast interpretation, risk reading, time boundaries, or model-quality signals.

Example:
if the neighboring block changes the final class, split boundary, time cutoff, or feature contribution, this term helps identify which internal step caused that change.

Up / Sideways / Down are the three classes of the [[current-prediction-daily-layer|Daily]] model.

What it shows:
[[landing-day-up|Up]] means the model expects an upward move above [[min-move|MinMove]]. [[landing-day-down|Down]] means a downward move below that threshold. [[landing-day-flat|Sideways]] means the day does not reach a meaningful move in either direction.

How to read it:
these classes do not describe final profit or loss yet. They describe the type of day the model sees before [[policy|Policy]] rules, filters, and risk layers are applied.

Example:
if the day is classified as [[landing-day-flat|sideways]], that is not a model error. It means the market did not show enough movement for an entry into a trade.

micro.title: Micro model
micro.text: The micro model is a separate model that activates only after [[current-prediction-daily-layer|Daily]] has classified the day as [[landing-day-flat|sideways]]. It does not replace Daily; it refines a weak tilt inside an otherwise neutral day.
notes: For [[landing-day-up|up]] and [[landing-day-down|down]] outputs, the micro model is not applied.
For [[landing-day-flat|sideways]] outputs, the micro model can refine a weak tilt up or down.
This helps preserve weak but repeatable directional signals inside days that Daily would otherwise leave neutral.

terms.description:
When the micro model is enabled — only after [[current-prediction-daily-layer|Daily]] has classified the day as [[landing-day-flat|sideways]].

What it shows:
the micro model is not used on days where Daily already chose [[landing-day-up|up]] or [[landing-day-down|down]]. Its working zone is limited to neutral days that do not cross [[min-move|MinMove]].

How to read it:
if a micro answer exists, it means the base daily layer did not see a full directional day and the stack needed extra refinement inside a sideways regime.

MicroTruth is the realized target used by the micro model inside [[landing-day-flat|sideways]] days.

What it shows:
it is the label that teaches the micro model to separate weak upward tilt from weak downward tilt among days that [[current-prediction-daily-layer|Daily]] first considered neutral.

How to read it:
MicroTruth does not replace the main daily truth. It only exists for the internal refinement of sideways days where a full directional signal was not strong enough for Daily.

Minimum data for micro is the condition that makes micro training possible at all.

What it shows:
the micro model needs enough [[landing-day-flat|sideways]] days and observations from both micro classes. Without that, it cannot learn the difference between weak upside and weak downside tilt.

How to read it:
if there is not enough data for micro, the base [[current-prediction-daily-layer|Daily]] layer remains the only working layer and no extra refinement inside sideways days is built.

Why the micro layer exists — to avoid losing weak but repeatable directional signals inside [[landing-day-flat|sideways]] days.

What it shows:
without the micro model, those days would stay simply neutral. With the micro layer, the system can still detect that price tends to lean in one direction inside an otherwise flat day.

How to read it:
if the micro layer adds a tilt, that does not mean a fully strong directional day. It is only a refinement inside a weak borderline regime that [[current-prediction-daily-layer|Daily]] would not turn into a strong class on its own.
